steps.title: Identify the key requirement for significance
steps.content: To an unfamiliar audience, significance means showing not just what changed, but why it mattered—what problem the Act solved.

steps.title: Match the requirement to the choices
steps.content:
Choice D alone connects the Act's abolition to the system's flaw—it favored northern Europe—showing the audience why the Act mattered.

Choices A, B, and C provide historical facts (quotas, 1890 baseline, that change occurred) without explaining why these facts demonstrate significance.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
